name: Marriott Bonvoy Brilliant® Card
effective_cost: Depends on hotel loyalty and free-night use

verdict.text: Keep if you're loyal to Marriott and actually book the free night

earn_note: The 6× hotel rate only applies at Marriott-participating properties; this card earns like a mid-tier card everywhere else. It's built around hotel loyalty, not general spend.

redemption_options.method: Transfer to airline miles (3:1, +5,000 bonus per 60,000 transferred)

points.note: Bonvoy points are worth the most redeemed for a free night at a hotel you'd actually book. Cashing out or transferring to airline miles both land well below that. The $800/100k figure assumes a typical free-night redemption, not a guarantee.

transfer_partners.highlight: Marriott Bonvoy transfers to 40+ airline programs at 3:1, with a 5,000-mile bonus for every 60,000 points transferred in a single transaction, but it doesn't transfer to any other hotel program, since it already is one.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: No confirmed recent changes to the airline transfer chart.

credits.name: Brilliant Dining Credit
credits.subtitle: $25/mo
credits.description: Up to $25/month in statement credits for purchases at restaurants worldwide.
tips: No enrollment required; just use the card at a restaurant.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck Credit
credits.subtitle: $120 GE or $85 TSA · every 4 years
credits.description: Statement credit for a Global Entry ($120) or TSA PreCheck ($85) application fee, once every 4 years. Shown here amortized to an annual figure.
tips: Apply for Global Entry, not just TSA PreCheck; it includes TSA PreCheck and covers the same credit.

credits.name: Ritz-Carlton / St. Regis Property Credit
credits.subtitle: $100 · 2-night minimum, direct booking
credits.description: Up to $100 property credit for qualifying charges at The Ritz-Carlton or St. Regis when booking a 2+ night stay direct at a special rate.
tips: Only pays off if you're already booking a Ritz-Carlton or St. Regis stay; not a general-purpose credit.

credits.name: Annual Free Night Award
credits.subtitle: up to 85,000-point category · after renewal month
credits.description: One free night award every year after your card renewal month, redeemable at hotels with a redemption level at or under 85,000 Marriott Bonvoy points.
tips: Value swings hard by property. A Category 1 redemption wastes most of the award's ceiling; aim for a hotel priced near the 85k cap.
$300 here is a conservative mid-range estimate, not what any specific hotel actually costs.

insurance.detail: Car Rental Loss & Damage Insurance, up to $75,000, secondary, no liability coverage
$3,000 combined ($2,000 checked / $1,000 carry-on) · $500 sub-limit on electronics/jewelry/watches

protection_note: Trip cancellation coverage matches Amex's most premium travel cards ($10k/trip), but trip delay is thinner ($500 vs $500-plus on some cards, capped at 2 claims/yr). This card leans on hotel loyalty perks, not being the strongest travel-protection card in your wallet.
rental_note: Rental coverage is secondary and excludes liability; put the rental on a card with primary coverage if you rent often, and use this one for the points instead.

status_perks.name: Marriott Bonvoy Platinum Elite Status
status_perks.note: Complimentary with the card: includes room upgrades (space-available), 25% bonus points, and enhanced guarantees. Plus 25 Elite Night Credits/year toward the next status tier.

status_perks.name: Priority Pass Select
status_perks.note: Unlimited visits to 1,200+ airport lounges worldwide, enrollment required.

additional_cards.title: Additional Cards

benefits.text: Free for up to 3 additional cards
Earns Marriott Bonvoy points on their spend (into your account)
Does not get their own Free Night Award or Elite status

additional_cards.note: Additional cards are free for the first three, a cheap way to pool household spend toward the 6× hotel rate and the annual free night.
